# web-scraper

> Standalone Python web scraper and browser control, extracted from the BACH system. Fetch pages, extract links and forms, inspect headers, clean main content, and capture screenshots.

Canonical repository: https://github.com/ellmos-ai/web-scraper
License: MIT
Language: Python
Runtime: Python >= 3.10

## Summary

web-scraper is a lightweight, dependency-free (by default) Python tool and library for web scraping and content extraction. It was extracted from the BACH system's `web_scrape.py` module to serve as a standalone utility. 

It provides six core operations:
1. `get`: Fetch page body (truncated to 10k preview characters by default).
2. `links`: Extract all deduplicated absolute links, filtering out mailto, tel, javascript, and anchor links.
3. `forms`: Parse forms, form actions, methods, and fields.
4. `headers`: Retrieve full response headers.
5. `extract`: Extract main text/Markdown content, stripping out boilerplate navigation, scripts, and styling.
6. `screenshot`: Save a PNG screenshot of a page (requires selenium and Chrome webdriver).

### Backend & Upgrades
The tool runs purely on Python's standard library (urllib + html.parser/regex) out of the box. Optional package upgrades improve robust behavior:
- `requests`: improved HTTP client (redirect, SSL fallback).
- `beautifulsoup4`: clean parsing of links, forms, and elements.
- `trafilatura`: advanced Markdown main content extraction.
- `selenium`: page rendering and screenshots.

### Security
To prevent Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF), all operations resolve target hosts and block private, loopback, link-local, multicast, and unspecified IP ranges unless explicit permission is granted via `allow_private=True`.

## CLI Usage

```bash
web-scraper get        https://example.com
web-scraper links      https://example.com
web-scraper forms      https://example.com
web-scraper headers    https://example.com
web-scraper extract    https://example.com          # Extracts clean Markdown
web-scraper screenshot https://example.com --out shot.png
```

Pass `--json` to output raw data dictionary. Disable SSL verification with `--no-verify-ssl` or allow private/loopback connections with `--allow-private`.

## Audience

- AI coding agents and LLM tools requiring headless content extraction without setting up heavy headless browsers.
- Python developers looking for a lightweight scraper that gracefully downgrades to Python stdlib.
- DevOps and automation scripts verifying endpoint response bodies and headers with built-in SSRF protection.

## Search Phrases

```
python lightweight web scraper stdlib
ssrf protected web scraping library python
headless markdown content extraction python
web-scraper get links forms headers
urllib HTML parser regex fallback scraper
trafilatura python BeautifulSoup fallback
extract main content webpage markdown python
ellmos web-scraper python
```

## External Discovery Notes

- The package is named `web-scraper` and lives under the `ellmos-ai` organization.
- It differentiates from other scrapers by its strict "zero dependencies by default" rule, and its native SSRF protection guard blocking localhost/internal subnets.
- Good discovery terms: local-first scraping, LLM-ready markdown extraction, lightweight browser helper, SSRF-guarded fetcher.

## Important Files

- `README.md`: English documentation.
- `README_de.md`: German documentation.
- `WIRING.md`: System wiring details and architecture.
- `RELEASE_GATE.md`: Release readiness checklist.
- `SECURITY.md`: Security policy and vulnerability reports.
- `TODO.md`: Project status and overall roadmap.
- `pyproject.toml`: package metadata and installation dependencies.
- `web_scraper/core.py`: core implementation containing WebScraper.
- `tests/test_web_scraper.py`: offline-only unit tests.
